action: CONTENT-vs-CHROME FALSE POSITIVE (soak #4 cycle 314, 2026-06-13): the codex-tmux auto-responder's rate_limit interactive-prompt rule (internal/bridge/manifests/codex-tmux.json: regex (usage|rate)[ -]limit (reached|exceeded|hit)|hit your (usage|rate) limit|too many requests|quota exceeded, policy=escalate) matched the AGENT'S OWN GENERATED CODE in the captured pane, not codex's actual rate-limit chrome. The cycle-314 task was clihealth coverage; the agent wrote clihealth_test.go:224 a pane fixture string 'You've hit your usage limit. Upgrade to Pro...' for clihealth's OWN rate-limit parser, and the bridge, capturing the full codex pane (scrollback body included), saw 'hit your usage limit' and concluded codex was rate-limited. Result: escalation-report written (pattern=rate_limit), session killed exit=85, fallback to claude-tmux, AND codex FAMILY BENCHED 30min (.evolve/cli-health.json reason=rate_limit strikes=1) removing the cross-family adversarial floor (builder codex->claude) for the whole batch and doubling claude load (bridge-timeout risk). SELF-REINFORCING: the batch goal steers toward clihealth (the rate-limit-detection package), so every clihealth cycle re-benches codex. The agent pane showed 'Working (1m 18s, esc to interrupt)' so codex was demonstrably HEALTHY. Additional defect: the bench evidence field recorded a later diff frame ('53 + Family: codex,') not the line that actually matched (match-frame != bench-record-frame). Fix directions: (1) scope the rate_limit detector to the CLI STATUS/FOOTER chrome region (where codex prints its real banner) instead of the full scrollback body - same content-vs-chrome lesson as the ADR-0026 busy-pane fix and ADR-0045 neutralized-pane_substr poisoning; (2) exclude diff-prefixed (+/-) and quoted-string-literal lines from CLI-state pattern matching; (3) require the rate_limit match to PERSIST across >=2 captures AND co-occur with an idle (non-Working) pane; (4) record the actual matching line as bench evidence. TDD: a pane fixture containing the clihealth test string in the scrollback BODY while the footer shows 'Working' must NOT escalate or bench; a real codex rate-limit banner in the footer still must. STATUS 2026-06-13: Layer-0 fix SHIPPED interactively — stripAgentDiffLines removes numbered-diff editor lines (`^[ \t]*\d+[ \t]+[+-]`) before decideAutoRespond matches any interactive-prompt regex; false codex bench cleared. REMAINING (this item stays open): bare unified-diff lines (`+content`/`-content` without line numbers) and quoted-string CONTEXT lines still slip through; the principled fixes (footer/status-chrome scoping; require persistence across >=2 captures + non-Working pane; record the actual matching line as bench evidence) are still unbuilt. Keep open for a proper bridge cycle.
evidence_pointer: internal/bridge/manifests/codex-tmux.json (rate_limit rule); internal/bridge/autorespond.go (tick matches full CapturePane); .evolve/runs/cycle-314/escalation-report.json; .evolve/worktrees/cycle-314/go/internal/clihealth/clihealth_test.go:224; .evolve/cli-health.json (codex benched strikes=1); cmd/evolve/cli_health_canary_test.go:75; ADR-0026 busy-pane + cycle-283 codex-usage-limit lesson
